title: Conversation Memory & History Flow
activity_flow: START: Caller sets up conversation context
BRANCH: New conversation → call agent.forUser(user) → sets conversationUser, clears conversationId
BRANCH: Continue existing → call agent.continue(conversationId, user) → sets both fields
BRANCH: Continue last → call agent.continueLastConversation(user) → ConversationStore.latestConversationId(user.id) → sets conversationId
DECISION: Is agent queued?
YES → agent.promptLater() → creates InvokeAgent job → wrapped in QueuedAgentResponse
→ Caller can register: QueuedAgentResponse.then(Closure) → delegates to InvokeAgent.then() → wraps in SerializableClosure → appended to thenCallbacks[]
→ Caller can register: QueuedAgentResponse.catch(Closure) → delegates to InvokeAgent.catch() → wraps in SerializableClosure → appended to catchCallbacks[]
→ Job dispatched to queue
→ InvokeAgent.handle() called by worker → withCallbacks(fn → agent.prompt(...)) executes
→ On success: iterates thenCallbacks[], calls each callback(response)
→ On failure: InvokeAgent.failed(Throwable) → iterates catchCallbacks[], calls each callback(e)
NO → agent.prompt() → synchronous execution
PIPELINE: GeneratesText.prompt(AgentPrompt) starts
→ gatherMiddlewareFor(agent) checks if agent uses RemembersConversations AND hasConversationParticipant()
DECISION: Has conversation participant?
YES → prepend RememberConversation middleware to pipeline
NO  → skip conversation middleware
→ pipeline runs through middleware then core handler
CORE HANDLER (inside pipeline then-closure):
→ agent is Conversational? → call agent.messages()
→ DECISION: conversationId set?
YES → ConversationStore.getLatestConversationMessages(conversationId, maxMessages=100) → returns Collection<Message>
NO  → return []
→ append new UserMessage(prompt, attachments) to messages
→ call textGateway.generateText(...messages...) → returns raw response with steps/toolCalls
→ build AgentResponse (or StructuredAgentResponse) with steps, toolCalls, toolResults
REMEMBER CONVERSATION MIDDLEWARE (post-response, via .then callback):
→ DECISION: agent.currentConversation() is null?
YES (new conversation):
→ generateTitle(prompt) → calls provider cheapest model for 3-5 word title
→ ConversationStore.storeConversation(userId, title) → inserts into agent_conversations → returns UUID
→ agent.continue(newConversationId, participant) → updates agent state
NO → skip creation
→ ConversationStore.storeUserMessage(conversationId, userId, AgentPrompt) → inserts user role record
→ ConversationStore.storeAssistantMessage(conversationId, userId, AgentPrompt, AgentResponse) → inserts assistant role record with toolCalls/toolResults/usage/meta
→ response.withinConversation(conversationId, participant) → attaches conversation context to response
END: AgentResponse returned to caller (contains conversationId for future calls)
key_relationships: RemembersConversations trait used_by Agent classes (e.g. AnonymousAgent via Promptable)
QueuedAgentResponse.then/catch delegates to InvokeAgent.then/catch via dispatchable.getJob()
InvokeAgent.withCallbacks() wraps agent.prompt() call and iterates thenCallbacks on success
Step data class holds: text, toolCalls[], toolResults[], finishReason, usage, meta (part of AgentResponse)
state_managed_by_RemembersConversations: conversationId: ?string — null for new, UUID for existing
conversationUser: ?object — the participant; presence gates memory middleware
forUser(user): starts fresh conversation for user
continueLastConversation(user): auto-resolves latest conversation from store
messages(): loads up to maxConversationMessages() (default 100) from store
hasConversationParticipant(): bool — gate used by GeneratesText middleware injection
queued_callback_mechanics: Callbacks are serialized via SerializableClosure before being stored on the job
FakePendingDispatch check in HasQueuedResponseCallbacks prevents callback registration during testing
catchCallbacks[] invoked with (Throwable $e) on job failure
database_tables: agent_conversations: id (UUID7), user_id, title, created_at, updated_at
agent_conversation_messages: id (UUID7), conversation_id, user_id, agent (class), role (user|assistant), content, attachments (JSON), tool_calls (JSON), tool_results (JSON), usage (JSON), meta (JSON)
important_notes: RememberConversation middleware runs post-response (inside .then on the pipeline response). Title generation uses a cheap AI model and falls back to truncated prompt on failure. AnonymousAgent implements Agent+Conversational+HasTools and uses Promptable trait which likely includes RemembersConversations. Step objects are nested inside AgentResponse.steps[] and capture per-step LLM outputs.
